# Add --extra-image-nvrs option to release-from-fbc pipeline#2663

## 

Add --extra-image-nvrs option to release-from-fbc pipeline#2663

ashwindasr wants to merge 2 commits intoopenshift-eng:mainopenshift-eng/art-tools:mainfrom 

ashwindasr:extra-image-nvrsashwindasr/art-tools:extra-image-nvrsCopy head branch name to clipboard

## Conversation

@ashwindasr 

 Copy link  Copy Markdown 

Contributor 

### 

@ashwindasr **ashwindasr**  commentedMar 26, 2026 • 

 edited 

Loading 

###  Uh oh!

There was an error while loading. Please reload this page.

## Summary

* Add `--extra-image-nvrs` CLI option to `artcd release-from-fbc` for extra image NVRs not part of the FBC
* Make `--fbc-pullspecs` optional (at least one of `--fbc-pullspecs` or `--extra-image-nvrs` must be provided)
* Extra NVRs are merged into the **same** image shipment file as FBC-related images (not a separate shipment)
* Skip FBC extraction/validation when no FBC pullspecs are provided (extra NVRs only)
* Validate extra image NVRs are not FBC builds before merging
* Add unit tests for init, categorization, and parameter handling

## Context

Sometimes we need to release builds that are not part of an FBC alongside the FBC and its related builds. The extra image NVRs are appended to `categorized_nvrs['image']` so they share the same image snapshot and shipment file as the FBC-extracted related images.

## Depends on

* Add EXTRA\_IMAGE\_NVRS parameter to release-from-fbc job aos-cd-jobs#4623 for the Jenkins `EXTRA_IMAGE_NVRS` parameter

Changes Cohort / File(s) Summary **Pipeline Core** pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/release\_from\_fbc.py Added optional extra\_image\_nvrs parameter to ReleaseFromFbcPipeline.\_\_init\_\_. run() now extends image NVRs with extra\_image\_nvrs, skips FBC/related-image validation when fbc\_pullspecs is empty, and only errors if both extracted NVRs and extra\_image\_nvrs are empty. CLI \--fbc-pullspecs made optional (default ""), \--extra-image-nvrs added, parsing updated, and command validation requires at least one source. **Tests** pyartcd/tests/pipelines/test\_release\_from\_fbc.py New test module verifying ReleaseFromFbcPipeline initialization handling of extra\_image\_nvrs (defaults, provided list, None → \[\]), retention of fbc\_pullspecs, and categorization behavior; includes a mocked elliottlib.util.extract\_nvrs\_from\_fbc. Inline comments:
In `@pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/release_from_fbc.py`:
- Around line 593-596: The extra_image_nvrs list is not validated before being
merged into categorized_nvrs['image'], which allows malformed NVRs or *-fbc
builds to slip through; before calling
categorized_nvrs['image'].extend(self.extra_image_nvrs) validate each entry in
self.extra_image_nvrs by running the same parsing/validation used by
categorize_nvrs()/parse_nvr() (or canonicalize them), filter out or raise on
invalid NVRs (and reject *-fbc builds), and only extend with the
normalized/validated NVRs so downstream elliott snapshot/new and shipment
generation receive correct values.

---

exercise the new run() and CLI control flow so changes in run() and
release_from_fbc() aren’t missed. Add one test in TestReleaseFromFbcCategorize
that constructs a pipeline with extra_image_nvrs (use _make_pipeline) and calls
run() to assert the extra NVRs are merged into the final output (verify behavior
of ReleaseFromFbcPipeline.run and release_from_fbc path), and add a second test

point/release_from_fbc function names) to locate where to add these tests.

pyartcd/pyartcd/pipelines/release\_from\_fbc.py 

 Comment on lines +778 to  +781 

| \# Merge extra image NVRs into the image category so they end up in the same image shipment   |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| if self.extra\_image\_nvrs:                                                                   |
| self.logger.info(f"Adding {len(self.extra\_image\_nvrs)} extra image NVRs to image shipment") |
| categorized\_nvrs\['image'\].extend(self.extra\_image\_nvrs)                                  |

 Copy link  Copy Markdown 

Contributor 

### 

@coderabbitai **coderabbitai bot** Mar 26, 2026 

There was a problem hiding this comment.

### Choose a reason for hiding this comment

 The reason will be displayed to describe this comment to others. Learn more.

 Choose a reason Spam Abuse Off Topic Outdated Duplicate Resolved Hide comment 

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Deduplicate merged image NVRs before building the snapshot.**

`categorized_nvrs["image"]` can already contain an NVR discovered from the FBC path, and `--extra-image-nvrs` can repeat it again or repeat within itself. `create_snapshot()` writes that merged list verbatim, so the generated snapshot/shipment will carry duplicate NVRs.

💡 Proposed fix 

         if self.extra_image_nvrs:
             self.logger.info(f"Adding {len(self.extra_image_nvrs)} extra image NVRs to image shipment")
-            categorized_nvrs['image'].extend(self.extra_image_nvrs)
+            categorized_nvrs["image"] = list(
+                dict.fromkeys([*categorized_nvrs["image"], *self.extra_image_nvrs])
+            )

🤖 Prompt for AI Agents 

```

merged image NVR list can contain duplicates because categorized_nvrs['image']
may already include entries also passed via self.extra_image_nvrs and
create_snapshot() writes the merged list verbatim; fix by deduplicating before
snapshot creation: after extending categorized_nvrs['image'] with
self.extra_image_nvrs (or instead of extending), produce a de-duplicated list
that preserves order (e.g., use an order-preserving dedupe like
list(dict.fromkeys(...)) or similar) and assign that back to
categorized_nvrs['image'] so create_snapshot() receives a unique set of image
NVRs.

```

<user_query>
https://github.com/openshift-eng/art-tools/pull/2663  rebase this PR
</user_query>
Checking git state in art-tools and rebasing the PR branch onto the latest main.
Reading conflicted files to resolve merge conflicts.
Resolving conflicts by merging main's `jira_bugs` and `target_release_date` with the PR's `extra_image_nvrs`, then fixing the test file.

Here is what was done:

## Rebase complete

`extra-image-nvrs` was rebased onto **`prod/main`** (current `openshift-eng/art-tools` `main`).

### Conflicts resolved

1. **`release_from_fbc.py` – `__init__` and CLI**  
   `main` added `jira_bugs` and `target_release_date`; your PR added `extra_image_nvrs`. All three are kept: constructor and `release_from_fbc()` pass **`jira_bugs`**, **`target_release_date`**, and **`extra_image_nvrs`**.

2. **`test_release_from_fbc.py`**  
   Kept `main`’s large test suite (JIRA, MR approvers, GitLab, etc.) and appended your **`TestReleaseFromFbcPipelineInit`**, **`TestCategorizeNvrs`**, and **`TestCliValidation`** classes.

3. **Second commit (`fix lint`)**  
   Import-order conflict fixed by keeping a single import block: `elliottlib` models plus `ReleaseFromFbcPipeline` and `_normalize_release_date`.

4. **Extra change**  
   When merging extra image NVRs into `categorized_nvrs['image']`, the list is **deduplicated in order** with `dict.fromkeys([...])` (same idea as the CodeRabbit note on the PR).

### New history (vs `prod/main`)

- `3983b4cdf` – Add `--extra-image-nvrs` option to release-from-fbc pipeline  
- `17bac8081` – fix lint  

### Tests

`pyartcd/tests/pipelines/test_release_from_fbc.py`: **42 passed**.

### Update the GitHub PR

Your local branch has diverged from `origin/extra-image-nvrs` because of the rebase. Publish with a force push to the branch that backs [PR #2663](https://github.com/openshift-eng/art-tools/pull/2663):

```bash
git push origin extra-image-nvrs --force-with-lease
```

(Use your usual remote name if it is not `origin`.) After the push, the **needs-rebase** label should clear once CI runs again.

<user_query>
push to origin
</user_query>

The user wants to push to origin. They're on the extra-image-nvrs branch after the rebase. I need to run git push origin extra-image-nvrs --force-with-lease since they rebased and need to update the remote branch.

<user_query>
wha does force-with-lease do
</user_query>

`git push --force-with-lease` updates the remote branch to match your local branch **the same way a normal `--force` would**, but with one safety check:

**It refuses to push if the remote branch has new commits that you don’t have locally.**

So:

- **`git push --force`** — Overwrites the remote branch with yours no matter what. If someone else pushed in between, you can wipe their commits without noticing.

- **`git push --force-with-lease`** — Still overwrites the remote (because your history was rewritten, e.g. after a rebase), **only if** the tip of the remote branch is still what your Git last saw (e.g. from `fetch`/`pull`). If the remote moved (new commits), the push fails and you have to `fetch`, reconcile, and try again.

**In short:** “Force my branch up there, but don’t clobber work I haven’t seen yet.” It’s the usual choice after a rebase when updating a PR branch.
